tldr: Cody is an AI coding assistant from Sourcegraph and AppLovin MAX is a mobile ad mediation SDK; they are not substitutes. The right plan is to decommission Cody across IDEs and Sourcegraph, and separately adopt AppLovin MAX inside your mobile apps.
why_switch: Removing an in-IDE AI coding assistant from the workflow
Reallocating tooling spend toward mobile monetisation
Adopting AppLovin MAX as part of a mediation strategy
Reducing AI provider footprint in the developer workflow
Internal policy change disallowing AI coding assistants
what_to_export: Cody workspace and IDE extension settings
Sourcegraph workspace and Cody account configuration
Custom prompts, commands and rules used by the team
Conversation history valuable for audit or knowledge transfer
List of users with Cody enabled in each IDE
Any tenant-level settings or admin policies
export_method: Inventory every IDE where Cody is installed (VS Code, JetBrains, web, etc.) and every user with access in your Sourcegraph workspace. From the Sourcegraph admin area, export tenant-level configuration including any Cody policies and connected code hosts. Ask developers to back up their custom prompts, commands and any local Cody settings to a shared location. Capture conversation history that is useful for audit. Once configuration is archived, plan to disable Cody at the workspace level and uninstall the IDE extensions. There is no application data to move; treat this as a controlled offboarding rather than a migration.
import_method: AppLovin MAX is integrated inside iOS, Android, Unity or React Native apps. Create an AppLovin account, register each app, and create ad units for the formats you intend to use. Integrate the AppLovin MAX SDK in each app following the official MAX integration guide, initialise it at app start and place ads at the chosen surfaces. Configure mediation networks in the MAX dashboard and update app-ads.txt with the required entries. Wire IAB TCF / GPP consent so ad requests carry the right privacy signals. Validate on real devices using MAX test mode, then release through standard app store updates.

concept_mapping.source: Cody IDE extension and Sourcegraph workspace
concept_mapping.target: AppLovin MAX mobile SDK and dashboard
concept_mapping.note: different product categories, no direct mapping

effort: 1-2 weeks to retire Cody; 3-8 weeks per platform to adopt AppLovin MAX
gotchas: Do not present this as a one-for-one migration; the products solve unrelated problems.
Disable Cody at the Sourcegraph workspace level, not only in IDEs, to fully revoke access.
Cancel the Cody subscription per the contract terms; uninstalling the extension does not stop billing.
AppLovin MAX integration changes the binary and triggers iOS and Android store reviews.
Ads introduce compliance obligations (privacy policy, consent prompts) that did not exist with Cody.
Missing or wrong app-ads.txt or consent setup silently reduces MAX revenue.

email.subject: Internal: retiring Cody, adopting AppLovin MAX
email.body:
Hi,

In the next 14 days we are retiring Cody as our in-IDE AI coding assistant and, in parallel, integrating AppLovin MAX into our mobile apps for ad monetisation. These two changes are independent.

If you use Cody today, you will be asked to uninstall it from your IDEs; access will also be revoked at the Sourcegraph workspace level. The AppLovin MAX integration ships through normal app store updates.

Reply with any questions about how this affects your work.

Thanks,
Your customer
